reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.25 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: The attendance table on page 2 of the source PDF lists approximately 23 individuals (Committee Members and Staff) across multiple rows. The corresponding table in the JSON output (index 7 in the second section) contains only the header row and zero data rows. This represents a loss of over 95% of the table's rows, which is a major defect.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The Attendance table on page 2 lists 12 Committee Members (from Commissioner Marty Cooke to Donna Wright) and 11 Staff members (from Richard Bradford to Angie Turbeville).
concerns.output_quote: The 'table' element with caption 'Attendance' (index 7 in Section 2) contains only one row marked as 'is_header_row': true, with no subsequent data rows.
concerns.location: Section 'Standards Meeting Minutes January', page 2, table index 7.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: The 'Roll Call Vote' data on page 4 of the source PDF, which includes names of committee members and their 'Y' votes for two policies, is entirely missing from the structured text and table content of the JSON. While a heading 'Roll Call Vote:' and an image of the table are present, the actual text/tabular information (names and results) is omitted from the machine-readable content. This constitutes an omission of nearly 100% of the information on that page, which is a major defect within that s
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The Roll Call Votes table on page 4 containing names (e.g., Commissioner Marty Cooke, Greg Coltrain) and their votes ('Y' in both columns).
concerns.output_quote: The JSON output for this section (Page 4 content) contains only a heading 'Roll Call Vote:' with no following table or text data.
concerns.location: Section 'Standards Meeting Minutes January', page 4, index 11.

concerns.analysis: There are significant structural table failures. The 'Attendance' table on page 2 is only partially converted; the structured 'table' element in Section 2 contains only the header row ('Committee Members', 'In Person', etc.), missing all 24 data rows present in the source. Furthermore, the 'Roll Call Votes' table on page 4 is completely missing as a structured table element, appearing only as an image in Section 2. This represents a failure to preserve the tabular organization of the document.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The Attendance table on page 2 contains 4 columns and approximately 24 data rows (e.g., 'Commissioner Marty Cooke', 'Greg Coltrain'). The Roll Call Votes table on page 4 contains 4 columns and 11 rows of voting data.
concerns.output_quote: The 'table' element in Section 2 ('Attendance') contains only 1 row: 'rows': [ { 'cells': [...], 'is_header_row': true } ]. No data rows follow. There is no 'table' element corresponding to the Roll Call Votes on page 4.
concerns.location: Section 2, index 6 (table) and near index 11 (missing table)

concerns.analysis: The HTML output exhibits a major organizational failure. The content of PDF page 4 (Roll Call Votes table) is entirely omitted. Instead, the content of PDF page 5 (resignation letter from Jason P. Steward) is placed directly beneath the 'Roll Call Vote:' heading. This completely disrupts the visual flow and logical organization of the document, as the letter has no relation to the roll call vote topic.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: PDF page 4 contains a table titled 'ROLL CALL VOTES' followed by page 5 which contains a resignation letter.
concerns.output_quote: In the HTML screenshot, the heading 'Roll Call Vote:' is immediately followed by the text of the resignation letter starting with 'standards06212021' and 'Jason P. Steward, Director...'. The 'ROLL CALL VOTES' table is missing.

concerns.analysis: There are two major table rendering issues. First, the 'ROLL CALL VOTES' table from PDF page 4 is completely missing from the HTML output. Second, the attendance table from PDF page 2 is misrendered in the 'Standards Meeting Minutes January' section: it appears once with data, but then the table headers are repeated as an empty shell further down in the same section, indicating a broken rendering process.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: PDF Page 4 shows a 'ROLL CALL VOTES' table with members and 'Y' vote indicators.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML screenshot lacks the Roll Call Votes table entirely. Additionally, an empty table header row ('Committee Members | In Person | Via Teams | Not Present') appears under the 'Attendance' heading in the 'Standards Meeting Minutes January' section.
concerns.location: Roll Call Vote section and Standards Meeting Minutes January section

concerns.analysis: Meaningful logos are missing from the HTML output. Specifically, the NCDIT logo found at the top of PDF page 7 and the logo associated with the Roll Call Votes table on PDF page 4 are absent from the rendered HTML screenshot.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: PDF Page 7 and Page 4 contain the NCDIT/911 Board logos.
concerns.output_quote: In the HTML screenshot, the '2022 Standards Committee Dates' section and the area where the Roll Call Vote content should be lack these logos.
concerns.location: Roll Call Vote: section and 2022 Standards Committee Dates section

concerns.analysis: The HTML output contains visually duplicated table structures. In the 'Standards Meeting Minutes January' section, the attendance table from PDF page 2 is essentially rendered twice: once as a full table with data at the start of the section, and then again as an empty set of headers later in the same section.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: PDF Page 2 contains a single instance of the attendance table.
concerns.output_quote: In the 'Standards Meeting Minutes January' section, the attendance table headers ('Committee Members', 'In Person', 'Via Teams', 'Not Present') appear at the top of the section and are then repeated further down under the 'Attendance' subheading.

concerns.analysis: The reading order on page 2 is logically inverted in the conversion. In the source PDF, the document header (Logo and Title) appears at the top, followed by the text 'Meeting Attendees:', and then the attendance table. In the output, the image of the attendance table is placed at the very beginning of the section, preceding the document's main title and the text label ('Meeting Attendees:') that is intended to introduce it. This breaks the logical flow of the information.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 2 top starts with logo/title 'North Carolina 911 Board Standards Committee Meeting Draft Minutes', then 'Meeting Attendees:', then the table.
concerns.output_quote: Section 2 elements start with an image of the attendance table (index 0), followed by the logo (index 1), then the title headings (index 2-3), and then the 'Meeting Attendees:' label (index 4).
